page: Microsoft Azure D96s_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance belonging to the DSv5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8370C processor with an x86_64 architecture, providing 96 dedicated virtual CPUs across 48 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The system is equipped with 384.0 GB of RAM, translating to a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of memory per core. This instance does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable, allowing for high-performance external disk attachments.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core capabilities and memory throughput. The instance achieves strong performance in multi-core CPU tests, software compilation, and ray tracing, alongside top-tier results in cryptographic encryption tests. Memory bandwidth is also highly rated, with read-only cached small block speeds reaching 5037.87 GB/sec and uncached large block write speeds at 131.45 GB/sec. However, single-core performance and text processing benchmarks fall into the average category. For LLM inference, while prompt processing for smaller models is average, text generation for large 70B models is weak, reflecting the lack of dedicated GPU acceleration.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D96s_v5 offers a dense allocation of compute resources and memory without the added cost of bundled local storage or specialized GPU hardware. This makes it a resource-dense option for workloads that scale horizontally or rely heavily on external network-attached storage. The instance is well-suited for multi-threaded applications, database operations, static web serving, and software compilation pipelines.
description: Microsoft Azure D96s_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the DSv5 family, powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor. It delivers 96 dedicated vCPUs across 48 physical cores, paired with 384.0 GB of RAM, maintaining a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-memory ratio. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance lacks local storage and GPUs but supports Premium Storage for external disk configurations. Benchmarks show strong multi-core processing, high memory bandwidth, and top-tier cryptographic performance, though single-core execution and large-scale LLM inference remain average to weak. Qualitatively, its high resource density provides a cost-effective platform for multi-threaded workloads without the premium of bundled local disks. It is highly suitable for database operations, web serving, and software compilation.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D96s_v5 is a general-purpose instance with 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 384 GB RAM. It offers strong multi-core and cryptographic performance for database and web serving workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D96s_v5 features 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 384 GB RAM, delivering strong multi-core performance for general-purpose workloads.
tagline: A high-core general-purpose instance featuring dedicated Intel processors and balanced memory configuration for demanding multi-threaded enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated vCPUs and 48 physical cores powered by Intel Xeon 8370C
384.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 1:4 resource ratio
Strong multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and cryptographic performance
Premium Storage capable with no local storage or GPU overhead
Optimized for database operations, web serving, and software compilation
